title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 10, 2026
headline: A floor-reinforcement and boundary-compression cycle: four entities confirmed at 0.0 or near-floor on war-crime and famine evidence — Myanmar's Bago monastery airstrike (28 civilians, possible war crime), Russia's 60,659 civilian casualties YTD, Israel's 70%-of-Gaza seizure, Sudan's conflict-famine constellation — while three corporate boundary cases compress further toward Critical; Oracle's WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window closes in five days.
summary: Tonight's cycle assessed twenty entities across four indexes — seventeen priority, three rotation — and confirmed every published score without generating a new scored proposal. The analytically significant work is concentrated in four domains. First, floor reinforcement: four entities at 0.0 absorb new evidence that deepens, but does not move, the active-perpetration designation. Myanmar's June 10 Fortify Rights report on the Bago monastery airstrike — 28 civilians killed including monks and community members, consistent with a war crime — confirms the same multi-category active-perpetration profile already priced at the absolute floor, now with a documented new atrocity event. Russia's 60,659 civilian casualties YTD (Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv strikes) and Sudan's conflict-famine constellation (2 million facing famine, 150,000+ killed) similarly deepen priced profiles without altering the floor designation. Second, near-floor confirmation: Iran at 2.5 (784+ executions YTD, one political prisoner executed every 49 hours — the highest execution rate in decades expressed as frequency), DRC at 2.3 (Ebola Bundibugyo 598 confirmed cases, 115 deaths — trajectory accelerating into the peak window), Ethiopia at 4.7 (repression-within-pattern continuation), Haiti at 4.7 (1.5 million IDPs, hurricane season active). Third, priced-continuation confirms: UAE at 18.4 (new Colombian-mercenary documentation reinforces the applied June 8 band-crossing without opening a new proposal — same-pattern-reinforcement discipline, external-complicity-by-proxy, ICJ unadjudicated), Bolivia at 18.4 (9th week of protests, 365+ arrested, military crackdown law patterns continuing without new impunity-law event), India at 15.6, US at 17.5 (ICE detention deaths and Fort Bliss pattern continuation), UnitedHealth at 10.2 (coordinated state-AG investigations still pre-adjudication). Fourth, boundary compression: Oracle at 20.6 (WARN Act window closes June 15 — five days), Uganda at 20.3 (Ebola reaching Kampala — WHO-coordinated response, natural-disaster-overlay intact), Cigna at 20.3 (rotation confirm, claim-denial profile). Anthropic confirmed at 59.1: IPO S-1 filing is a corporate-structure event, net-neutral on conduct — no behavior change, no oversight change, no harm reduction or amplification. The DC Circuit appeal and EU AI Act countdown remain the operative scoring pivots.

topSignals.title: Myanmar 0.0 — Bago Monastery Airstrike June 10: 28 Civilians Killed, Possible War Crime; Floor Reinforced
topSignals.description: The June 10 Fortify Rights report documents a Myanmar junta airstrike on a Bago monastery that killed 28 civilians including Buddhist monks and community members — an attack on a protected religious site with civilian concentrations, consistent with a war crime under international humanitarian law. The Bago monastery airstrike deepens the multi-category active-perpetration profile already priced at 0.0: the floor encodes systematic military targeting of civilian infrastructure (schools, hospitals, religious sites), forced displacement of 1.6 million, use of starvation as a weapon of war, and documented mass atrocity across Sagaing, Chin, and Karen states. The June 10 Bago event adds a new documented atrocity to the perpetration record but does not alter the floor designation, which is already at the absolute minimum. The floor cannot move lower.

topSignals.title: Russia 0.0 — 60,659 Civilian Casualties YTD; Kyiv, Dnipro, and Kharkiv Strikes Continue; Active-Perpetration Floor Reinforced
topSignals.description: Russia's YTD civilian casualty toll reached 60,659 (killed and wounded as documented by OHCHR), with concentrated June strikes on Kyiv, Dnipro, and Kharkiv targeting residential areas and critical civilian infrastructure. The trajectory represents a deepening of the active-perpetration record already encoded in the 0.0 floor: systematic deliberate targeting of civilian populations, weaponization of energy and water infrastructure, and documented commission of war crimes across the occupied territories. The YTD figure now exceeds the full-year 2024 total with more than six months remaining. The floor is fully constituted and cannot move lower.

topSignals.title: Israel 0.0 — 70% Gaza Seizure Order; 119+ June Deaths; Active-Perpetration Floor Reinforced
topSignals.description: Netanyahu's order to permanently seize approximately 70% of Gaza — 40 documented military outpost positions, 8 established post-ceasefire in violation of the October 2025 ceasefire agreement — alongside 119 Palestinians killed in June, humanitarian aid down 37% from allocation, and 1.8 million displaced confirms the active-perpetration floor at 0.0. The seizure order represents a structural-permanence marker that deepens the perpetration record beyond episodic military operations. External complicity evidence (UAE arming, Microsoft Azure/Unit 8200) is scored under those entities and not against this sovereign floor under no-double-count discipline. The floor cannot move lower.

topSignals.title: Sudan 0.0 — Conflict-Famine: 2 Million Facing Famine, 150,000+ Killed; Active-Perpetration Floor Reinforced
topSignals.description: Sudan's conflict-famine constellation deepens across three concurrent drivers: RSF-perpetrated genocide in Darfur (UN Fact-Finding Mission finding of genocide characteristics), SAF bombardment of civilian areas including Khartoum, and now IPC-confirmed famine affecting 2 million people — the direct outcome of deliberate humanitarian-access obstruction by both parties. The 150,000+ killed figure represents the largest absolute conflict-mortality toll of any active war globally. The active-perpetration floor at 0.0 encodes all dimensions at 1.0 with zero integration premium; UAE external-sponsorship of the RSF is scored separately under UAE and not against the Sudan floor under no-double-count discipline.

topSignals.title: Iran 2.5 — 784+ Executions YTD; One Political Prisoner Executed Every 49 Hours; Near-Floor Confirmed
topSignals.description: Iran's execution rate has reached the highest frequency in decades: 784+ documented executions YTD translates to one execution approximately every 49 hours across the full year-to-date period, including political prisoners and drug-offense capital sentences that fall below the international minimum threshold for capital punishment. Five political prisoners face imminent execution as of this cycle. The 'No to Executions Tuesdays' campaign continues its consecutive-week series across 56 prison facilities. The single-dominant-extremity hold at near-floor 2.5 is maintained: the multi-category absolute-0.0 threshold requires a second dominant harm category at comparable scale. Confirmed at 2.5.

topSignals.title: DRC 2.3 — Ebola 598 Cases / 115 Deaths; Bundibugyo Trajectory Accelerating Into Peak Window; Near-Floor Confirmed
topSignals.description: The DRC Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak has reached 598 confirmed cases and 115 deaths under the active WHO PHEIC — an acceleration of approximately 83 cases and 24 deaths from the June 9 cycle (515/91). Cross-border spread to Uganda is confirmed; contact tracing covers approximately 20% of known transmission chains across 17 of 36 Ituri health zones. No approved vaccine exists for the Bundibugyo strain. The concurrent humanitarian burden — M23/AFC conflict in eastern DRC, 26.5 million food-insecure, world's largest displacement crisis — compounds the epidemiological shock. The natural-disaster-overlay-non-double-count hold at near-floor 2.3 remains intact: deliberate documented state obstruction of the WHO response is the conversion trigger, not outbreak acceleration alone.

topSignals.title: Oracle Corporation 20.6 — WARN Act Window Closes June 15; Five Days to the Most Proximate Hard Scoring Trigger in the Current Cycle
topSignals.description: Oracle's WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window for approximately 30,000 affected workers closes June 15 — five days from this cycle. Oracle satisfied the 60-day WARN Act notice requirement by placing workers on paid administrative leave and absorbing WARN pay into severance, leaving workers excluded from the program with zero net payout. Affected populations include cancer patients, pregnant employees, H-1B visa holders at deportation risk, veterans, and workers within RSU vesting milestones. The remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory is active in the Washington state class action and Missouri state investigation. Oracle confirmed at 20.6 at 0.6 points above Critical; adverse adjudication on the avoidance theory after June 15 constitutes an immediate crossing trigger.

topSignals.title: Uganda 20.3 — Ebola Reaching Kampala; WHO-Coordinated Response Active; Boundary at 0.3 Points Above Critical
topSignals.description: Ebola Bundibugyo spread from DRC has reached Kampala, Uganda's capital and primary urban center — an urban escalation that qualitatively changes the outbreak's transmission risk profile. Nine confirmed Kampala-area cases have been documented, with WHO-coordinated response active. Uganda's 20.3 composite sits 0.3 points above Critical. The natural-disaster-overlay-non-double-count ruling holds: the Kampala escalation represents exogenous epidemiological shock, and the WHO-coordinated response demonstrates active state engagement rather than obstruction. Documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response — access denial, fund diversion, case-data suppression — remains the conversion trigger from the current hold.

topSignals.title: Anthropic 59.1 — IPO S-1 Filing Is Net-Neutral Corporate-Structure Event; DC Circuit Appeal Remains the Scoring Pivot
topSignals.description: Anthropic's IPO S-1 filing is a corporate-structure event — a capital-markets step — that does not constitute a conduct change, a harm reduction, or a harm amplification under the methodology. Corporate structure is not a compassion-dimension variable absent evidence of governance changes that materially affect safety commitments, oversight mechanisms, or deployment behavior. The DC Circuit appeal (Anthropic v. Hegseth on Pentagon supply-chain risk), the EU AI Act GPAI obligations (August 2 — 53 days from this cycle), and the Great American AI Act (bipartisan draft, not yet enacted) remain the operative scoring pivots. ASL-3 activation documentation and the METR agent-misalignment pilot are prospective safety positives with no adjudication or enacted obligation attached. Confirmed at 59.1 Functional.

topSignals.title: UAE 18.4 — New Colombian-Mercenary Documentation Reinforces Applied Score; Same-Pattern Discipline Holds
topSignals.description: June 10 documentation of additional Colombian-mercenary pipeline evidence — consistent with the Conflict Insights Group tracking and Desert Wolves brigade deployments documented in the June 8–9 cycle sequence — reinforces the applied June 8 band-crossing score (18.4, Developing → Critical, −5.0) without opening a new proposal. The June 10 evidence is the same active-complicity-by-proxy pattern: external sponsorship of the RSF, ICJ case unadjudicated (Sudan v. UAE, filed March 2025), no documented in-territory UAE perpetration. Per same-pattern-reinforcement discipline, the applied 18.4 is the operative record. Each additional documentation cycle without verified cessation of RSF arms and mercenary transfers deepens the record toward the ICJ adjudication threshold.

boundaryWatch.trigger: WARN Act last-working-day sign-or-forfeit window closes June 15 — five days. Washington state class action and Missouri state investigation on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory are both active adjudication channels. Adverse adjudication after June 15 constitutes an immediate scoring trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Oracle at 0.6 points above Critical entering the final five days before the WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window closes. The zero-net-payout structure for newer and excluded workers — cancer patients, pregnant employees, H-1B holders, veterans, RSU-milestone workers — compounds the EMP dimension record each day the window remains open without adjudication.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO Ebola response — access denial, fund diversion, case-data suppression — constitutes the conversion trigger from the natural-disaster-overlay hold. Kampala urban escalation does not by itself break the overlay; WHO-coordinated response remains active.
boundaryWatch.note: Uganda at 0.3 points above Critical as Ebola Bundibugyo reaches Kampala with nine confirmed urban cases. The WHO-coordinated response is active; Uganda's engagement rather than obstruction of WHO access is the basis for maintaining the natural-disaster-overlay hold. The urban escalation qualitatively raises the outbreak's scoring-observation priority through the peak window.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Adjudicated claim-denial finding or mental-health parity ruling would push into Critical from 0.3 points above the boundary. AZ AG eight-insurer cartel suit (naming Cigna alongside UnitedHealth, Aetna, and five others) is an active sector-wide adjudication channel.
boundaryWatch.note: Cigna at 0.3 points above Critical, confirmed at 20.3 this rotation cycle. The claim-denial and algorithmic-review profile driving the ACC/EQU 1.5 composite sits at the same narrow margin as the June 9 cycle. The AZ AG cartel suit adjudication, if reached, would simultaneously reprice seven other Fortune 500 health insurers.

boundaryWatch.trigger: ICJ ruling in Sudan v. UAE (filed March 2025); verified cessation of RSF arms and mercenary transfers is the primary reversal signal. Documented direct UAE-force participation in Sudan would move toward a floor assessment.
boundaryWatch.note: UAE's applied 18.4 sits 1.6 points above the Critical-band floor following the June 8 band crossing. June 10 mercenary documentation constitutes additional reinforcement of the applied score's evidence base — same active-complicity-by-proxy pattern, ICJ case unadjudicated, no in-territory UAE perpetration.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Judicial annulment or legislative repeal of the June 8 presumption-of-legality military-force law is the primary reversal signal. Continued military deployments under the signed law with documented casualties reinforce the applied 18.4 Critical baseline.
boundaryWatch.note: Bolivia at its applied 18.4 Critical score entering the 9th consecutive week of protest activity. The June 10 cycle documents continuing military crackdown patterns consistent with the CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION evidence base priced in the June 8 band crossing; no new event category emerged this cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Appellate review of the Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions. Reinstatement of the rescinded June 4 ICE post-release death-reporting requirement would be the first upward-direction indicator within the applied 17.5 Critical baseline.
boundaryWatch.note: United States at its applied 17.5 Critical score. June 10 cycle confirms ICE detention deaths and Fort Bliss pattern continuation at the priced ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor — same-regime continuation without a new un-priced harm category.

emergingRisks.risk: Myanmar's junta documents a new category of target — protected religious sites — with the Bago monastery airstrike; three prior atrocity patterns (schools, hospitals, civilian infrastructure) are already priced at the floor; the monastery category deepens the multi-domain perpetration record.
emergingRisks.detail: The Bago monastery airstrike represents a fourth documented target category (religious sites) alongside schools, hospitals, and residential infrastructure. While the floor cannot move lower from 0.0, each documented category deepens the accountability record and strengthens the evidentiary case for international justice mechanisms including ICC referrals and individual criminal accountability proceedings.

emergingRisks.risk: Oracle's WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window closes June 15 — five days from this cycle; Oracle sits 0.6 points above Critical with the Washington class action and Missouri investigation both active on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory.
emergingRisks.detail: The June 15 window closure is the hardest single-date calendar trigger in the current cycle sequence. The zero-net-payout structure for excluded workers accumulates EMP-dimension documentation with each cycle the window remains open. Adverse adjudication on the avoidance theory after June 15 constitutes an immediate crossing trigger from 0.6 points above Critical.

emergingRisks.risk: Ebola Bundibugyo has reached Kampala — Uganda's capital of 3.7 million — with nine confirmed urban cases under WHO PHEIC; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine exists and DRC contact-tracing covers only 20% of known transmission chains.
emergingRisks.detail: The Kampala urban escalation qualitatively changes the outbreak's risk profile: capital-city transmission with lower contact-tracing density, higher population mobility, and a health system that remains WHO-coordinated but is not at ZAIRE-strain preparedness levels. DRC at 598/115 accelerating into the July peak window. The natural-disaster-overlay holds as long as WHO access is maintained; documented obstruction is the crossing trigger.

emergingRisks.risk: Sudan's conflict-famine has reached IPC Phase 5 famine designation for 2 million people — the first confirmed famine in Sudan in decades — with both RSF and SAF parties using humanitarian access obstruction as a tool of war.
emergingRisks.detail: IPC Phase 5 famine designation for 2 million people is a milestone that typically anchors international humanitarian response escalation. Sudan's 0.0 floor already encodes active-perpetration including the humanitarian-obstruction dimension. The IPC famine finding strengthens the evidential record for international accountability mechanisms targeting both RSF and SAF leadership. UAE external sponsorship of RSF is scored separately.

emergingRisks.risk: The EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take effect August 2 — 53 days from this cycle — with no assessed AI lab having published required compliance documentation.
emergingRisks.detail: Anthropic's IPO S-1 filing does not address EU AI Act compliance. Non-compliance fines reach 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover. The Great American AI Act bipartisan draft adds mandatory twice-yearly audits and $1M/day penalties at the domestic level. Both instruments bear on the same compliance-documentation gap. August 2 is the nearer of the two hard deadlines.

emergingRisks.risk: North Korea's forced-labor documentation in EU supply chains now spans multiple sectors; Fortune 500 entities with opaque tier-2 and tier-3 supplier relationships face EMP and ACC scoring exposure when EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive audit obligations activate.
emergingRisks.detail: The June 10 scanner surfaced expanded North Korean forced-labor documentation across EU manufacturing supply chains including electronics, construction materials, and apparel. No individual Fortune 500 entity crossed a proposal threshold this cycle; the risk is tracked as a supply-chain transparency drift signal requiring entity-specific audit confirmation before scoring.

emergingRisks.risk: Bolivia's military crackdown law is now in its 9th consecutive week of protest-suppression application; the codified impunity pattern continues to accumulate documentation without a new headline event, raising the risk of a secondary atrocity that would trigger a new scored proposal.
emergingRisks.detail: Bolivia at 18.4 Critical following the June 8 band crossing. Nine weeks of protest and military deployment under the presumption-of-legality law creates a documented pattern of escalating state violence; a single documented mass-casualty event under the law attributable to state forces would constitute a new harm category beyond the priced 18.4 baseline.

emergingRisks.risk: India's Bengali-Muslim deportation pattern enters a new accountability context as the EU Returns Regulation takes force June 12 — two days from this cycle — potentially activating a refoulement-accountability signal if EU-facilitated returns occur without individual protection assessments.
emergingRisks.detail: India at 15.6 (applied June 1) has accumulated four cycles of same-regime-continuation evidence on the deportation pattern. Confirmed state-attributable enforced disappearances among deportees at scale would constitute a new harm category independently scorable beyond 15.6. The EU Returns Regulation entering force creates the accountability channel; documented application is the signal.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Floor Cohort: Four Active-Perpetration Floors Reinforced This Cycle
sectorAlerts.headline: Myanmar (Bago monastery airstrike — 28 civilians, possible war crime), Russia (60,659 casualties YTD), Israel (70% seizure order), and Sudan (IPC famine, 150,000+ killed) each absorb new evidence that deepens the 0.0 floor without altering the designation
observations: Myanmar 0.0: June 10 Fortify Rights report on Bago monastery airstrike — 28 civilians killed including monks, attack on protected religious site — adds a fourth documented target category to the multi-domain perpetration record alongside schools, hospitals, and residential infrastructure

Russia 0.0: 60,659 civilian casualties YTD (OHCHR) with concentrated June strikes on Kyiv, Dnipro, and Kharkiv; YTD figure already exceeds full-year 2024 total with six months remaining — perpetration rate accelerating relative to prior year

Israel 0.0: 70%-of-Gaza seizure order covers 40 military outpost positions (8 established post-ceasefire); structural-permanence marker distinguishes current cycle from episodic military operations; 119+ killed in June, aid down 37%

Sudan 0.0: IPC Phase 5 famine designation for 2 million people — first confirmed famine in Sudan in decades — combined with 150,000+ killed and RSF genocide finding constitutes the most severe compound humanitarian crisis in the current assessment universe
North Korea 0.0 carry-forward: forced-labor documentation in EU supply chains adds new sector dimension to the systematic-oppression floor record; floor itself unchanged

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Near-Floor Cohort: Iran, DRC, Ethiopia, Haiti Confirmed
sectorAlerts.headline: Near-floor entities confirm at published scores with deepening evidence; DRC Ebola trajectory accelerates with Kampala urban escalation as the most significant qualitative change
observations: Iran 2.5: 784+ executions YTD expressing as one execution every 49 hours — highest frequency expression of the single-dominant-extremity hold; five political prisoners face imminent execution; 'No to Executions Tuesdays' continues its consecutive-week series across 56 facilities

DRC 2.3: Ebola reaches 598/115 from 515/91 at June 9 — 83 case / 24 death increase in one cycle; Kampala urban escalation confirmed (nine cases); natural-disaster-overlay-non-double-count holds as WHO response remains active and unobstructed
Ethiopia 4.7: Post-election repression consolidation continues; EHPA suspension and press crackdown patterns deepen within the priced near-floor profile; no new un-priced mass-atrocity category
Haiti 4.7: 1.5 million IDPs, active hurricane season, gang-driven collapse at near-floor; non-state-harm driver maintains the floor-transition-rather-than-floor designation at 4.7

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Priced-Continuation Cluster: UAE, Bolivia, US, India Confirmed
sectorAlerts.headline: Four recently applied Critical-band scores confirm on same-regime-continuation evidence; UAE's June 10 mercenary documentation is the day's clearest example of the duplicate-suppression discipline applied to an already-committed score
observations: UAE 18.4 (applied June 8): June 10 Colombian-mercenary documentation is additional reinforcement of the applied score's evidence base — same active-complicity-by-proxy pattern, ICJ unadjudicated, no in-territory UAE perpetration; same-pattern-reinforcement discipline maintained

Bolivia 18.4 (applied June 9): 9th consecutive week of protest activity; military crackdown law patterns without a new impunity-law event; CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION evidence base unchanged — no re-litigation absent a new un-priced harm category

United States 17.5 (applied June 9): ICE detention deaths and Fort Bliss pattern at priced ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor; 17 custody deaths YTD continues to be the highest rate in two decades; same-regime continuation

India 15.6 (applied June 1): Bengali-Muslim deportation pattern at fourth cycle of same-regime continuation; EU Returns Regulation June 12 entry into force is the next forward trigger for a potential new accountability signal

sectorAlerts.sector: Fortune 500 — Boundary Compression: Oracle, Cigna Enter Final Pre-Trigger Window
sectorAlerts.headline: Oracle at 0.6 points above Critical with five days to the June 15 WARN Act window; Cigna confirmed at 0.3 points; three-entity corporate boundary cluster from June 9 narrows to two active corporate cases plus Uganda from the country index
observations: Oracle 20.6: WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window closes June 15 — five days; zero-net-payout structure for excluded workers (cancer patients, pregnant employees, H-1B holders, veterans, RSU-milestone workers); Washington class action and Missouri investigation on remote-reclassification avoidance theory both active

Cigna 20.3: Rotation confirm at 0.3 points above Critical; claim-denial and algorithmic-review profile drives ACC/EQU 1.5 composite; AZ AG eight-insurer cartel suit is the active adjudication channel — adjudication would simultaneously reach seven other Fortune 500 health insurers
3M Company 20.3 carry-forward: Australia $1.43B PFAS Phase-2 MDL July 31; boundary position unchanged from June 9; Wisconsin attribution boundary (Tyco/Johnson Controls) confirmed
The 3M/Oracle/Cigna triple boundary cluster from June 9 narrows slightly in daily observation priority: Oracle's June 15 hard trigger is now the dominant near-term corporate boundary event

sectorAlerts.sector: AI Labs — Corporate Structure vs. Conduct: Anthropic IPO S-1 Ruling
sectorAlerts.headline: Anthropic's IPO S-1 filing resolved as net-neutral corporate-structure event; the DC Circuit appeal and EU AI Act August 2 deadline remain the lab sector's operative scoring pivots for this cycle sequence
observations: Anthropic 59.1: IPO S-1 filing is a capital-markets corporate-structure step — no conduct change, no governance change, no harm reduction or amplification documented; IPO-CORPORATE-STRUCTURE-NET-NEUTRAL ruling applied; confirmed at 59.1 Functional

The ruling extends the corporate-structure-is-not-a-conduct-variable principle: capital raises, ownership structure changes, and public-market filings are methodology-neutral absent documented changes to safety commitments, deployment behavior, or oversight mechanisms

EU AI Act GPAI obligations effective August 2 (53 days): no assessed AI lab has published required transparency documentation; Anthropic's S-1 filing does not constitute EU AI Act compliance disclosure
Great American AI Act bipartisan draft at procedural stage: mandatory safety-framework publication, twice-yearly audits, $1M/day penalties; not yet enacted; net-neutral absent passage

DC Circuit appeal (Anthropic v. Hegseth on Pentagon supply-chain risk) remains the near-term lab-sector scoring pivot: favorable ruling triggers Functional/Established crossing; adverse ruling produces downward movement

sectorAlerts.sector: Ebola PHEIC — Kampala Urban Escalation: Most Significant Qualitative Change This Cycle
sectorAlerts.headline: Ebola Bundibugyo reaches Kampala (nine cases) as DRC outbreak accelerates to 598/115; no approved vaccine; WHO PHEIC active; the Kampala urban cluster represents the highest-consequence epidemiological escalation in the current cycle
observations: Uganda 20.3 boundary: Kampala urban cluster (nine confirmed cases) is the highest-risk geographic escalation since the outbreak began; capital-city transmission creates higher-density contact networks and lower contact-tracing efficiency than rural settings

DRC 2.3 near-floor: 598/115 from 515/91 at June 9 — an 83-case, 24-death single-cycle increase that is among the highest single-cycle jumps in the outbreak timeline; 17 health zones affected, contact-tracing at 20%
WHO PHEIC coordination framework is the primary barrier preventing the natural-disaster-overlay conversion trigger; both Uganda and DRC governments are documented as cooperating with WHO response

The Bundibugyo-specific absence of an approved vaccine means that the standard ring-vaccination rapid-containment protocol used for Zaire strain outbreaks is unavailable — limiting the intervention toolkit to contact-tracing, isolation, and supportive care
The July 31 peak window remains the structural review deadline for both entities; documented state obstruction before that date is the immediate conversion trigger

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Forced Labor and Supply Chain Transparency: North Korea EU Documentation
sectorAlerts.headline: Scanner surfaces expanded North Korean forced-labor documentation in EU supply chains across multiple sectors; no individual Fortune 500 entity crossed a proposal threshold but supply-chain transparency drift is flagged as a sector-wide monitoring signal
observations: North Korea 0.0 floor-state continues to generate supply-chain externalities in EU manufacturing via state-organized forced-labor export programs
Documentation spans electronics, construction materials, and apparel sectors — the broadest multi-sector coverage documented in the current cycle sequence

Fortune 500 entities with deep EU manufacturing exposure and opaque tier-2/tier-3 supplier relationships (Walmart, Boeing, Microsoft flagged as monitoring candidates) face potential EMP and ACC scoring exposure when EU CSDDD audit obligations activate

The EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive audit obligations, once in effect, would require entity-specific supply-chain audit findings before any scoring action — this cycle's documentation establishes the monitoring baseline

sectorAlerts.sector: ICE Detention and Immigration Enforcement — Fort Bliss Pattern Continuation
sectorAlerts.headline: US 17.5 cycle confirms the Fort Bliss GAO findings carry forward; 17 in-custody deaths YTD remains the highest rate in two decades; same-regime continuation at priced Critical baseline
observations: The Fort Bliss GAO report (three deaths including destroyed-evidence homicide, $18M wasted) from June 9 continues to anchor the ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor in the US 17.5 priced composite

17 ICE in-custody deaths YTD at the highest per-detention rate in more than two decades is a cumulative metric that grows each cycle without a reversal; the June 4 death-reporting rescission removes the transparency mechanism that would track continued accumulation

The rescission of the post-release death-reporting requirement is the most consequential structural change in US immigration enforcement accountability this cycle: it eliminates the data infrastructure for future death-rate adjudication
Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions under appellate review represent the primary near-term judicial accountability signal for the deportation pattern

methodologyNotes.name: IPO-CORPORATE-STRUCTURE-NET-NEUTRAL (Anthropic — first application)
methodologyNotes.description: An initial public offering S-1 filing is a corporate-structure and capital-markets event. Corporate structure is not a compassion-dimension variable under the methodology absent documented changes to governance mechanisms, safety commitments, deployment behavior, or institutional oversight that materially affect harm reduction or amplification. Anthropic's IPO S-1 filing documents capital raise mechanics, shareholder rights, and financial risk factors — none of which constitute scored conduct. The DC Circuit appeal (Anthropic v. Hegseth), EU AI Act compliance behavior, and Great American AI Act enactment remain the operative scoring variables. Applied as IPO-CORPORATE-STRUCTURE-NET-NEUTRAL: confirmed at 59.1 with no proposal generated.

methodologyNotes.name: NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT — Kampala Urban Extension (Uganda, DRC)
methodologyNotes.description: The Kampala urban escalation of the Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak (nine confirmed cases) does not break the natural-disaster-overlay hold established for Uganda and DRC. The overlay requires: (1) the primary harm driver is an exogenous epidemiological event, not a deliberate state act; and (2) there is no documented deliberate state obstruction of the international response. Both conditions hold this cycle: the Kampala cases represent geographic spread of an exogenous pathogen, and both Uganda and DRC governments are cooperating with WHO PHEIC response. Urban escalation increases the monitoring priority and raises the stakes of the conversion trigger but does not by itself constitute the trigger. Documented state obstruction (access denial, fund diversion, case-data suppression) remains the only conversion trigger.

methodologyNotes.name: SAME-PATTERN-REINFORCEMENT-ON-APPLIED-SCORE (UAE — third application)
methodologyNotes.description: When new evidence is assessed and confirmed as the same pattern underpinning an already-applied score — deepening documentation without adding a new un-priced harm category — no new proposal is generated and the applied score is the operative record. The June 10 UAE Colombian-mercenary documentation is the same active-complicity-by-proxy pattern applied in the June 8 band crossing (23.4 → 18.4). The ICJ case remains unadjudicated; no documented in-territory UAE perpetration is established; external-sponsorship characterization stands. This is the third application of the same-pattern-reinforcement discipline for the UAE in this cycle sequence (June 9 first application on open proposal; June 10 first application on applied score).

methodologyNotes.name: FLOOR-MULTI-CATEGORY-REINFORCEMENT (Myanmar — monastery target category)
methodologyNotes.description: The Bago monastery airstrike adds a fourth documented civilian target category (religious sites) to Myanmar's active-perpetration floor record alongside schools, hospitals, and residential infrastructure. Multi-category reinforcement of a 0.0 floor designation does not move the floor lower — the floor is already at the absolute minimum — but it deepens the accountability record. Each documented target category strengthens the evidentiary basis for international criminal accountability mechanisms (ICC referrals, individual criminal accountability). Floors exit only through verified cessation of active perpetration and documented accountability; multi-category reinforcement moves in the opposite direction on both dimensions.

methodologyNotes.name: ACTIVE-PERPETRATION-FLOOR-REINFORCEMENT (Russia, Israel, Sudan — re-confirmed June 10)
methodologyNotes.description: Russia (60,659 civilian casualties YTD; Kyiv/Dnipro/Kharkiv June strikes), Israel (70%-of-Gaza seizure order; 119+ June killed; ceasefire violation structural-permanence marker), and Sudan (IPC Phase 5 famine 2M; 150,000+ killed; RSF genocide finding) each absorb new cycle evidence that deepens the active-perpetration record without moving the 0.0 floor designation. All three floors were fully constituted in prior cycles; June 10 evidence is same-regime-continuation at the absolute floor. No-double-count discipline maintained: UAE external-sponsorship of RSF scored under UAE; Microsoft Azure/Unit 8200 scored under Microsoft; not against sovereign floors.

methodologyNotes.name: PRICED-CONTINUATION (Bolivia, US, India — re-confirmed June 10)
methodologyNotes.description: All three entities are at recently applied lower scores and the June 10 evidence is same-regime continuation already priced. Bolivia 18.4: 9th-week protest continuation under military crackdown law without a new impunity-law event. United States 17.5: ICE in-custody death rate continuation at priced ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor. India 15.6: Bengali-Muslim deportation pattern at fourth cycle of same-regime continuation; EU Returns Regulation June 12 is the next forward trigger. None of the three entities presents a new un-priced harm category requiring re-litigation.

methodologyNotes.name: FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED (UnitedHealth Group — re-confirmed June 10)
methodologyNotes.description: The coordinated state-AG investigation density for UnitedHealth Group continues to rise — now spanning four concurrent sovereign enforcement actions — but enforcement density is not adjudication. The June 10 cycle produces no new merits determination, settlement, or formal charge that would convert the pre-adjudication hold. CEO resignation (personal reasons) is governance churn without a compassion-harm signal. Confirmed at 10.2; conversion trigger remains a merits adjudication or settlement on any of the four actions.

methodologyNotes.name: ROTATION-CONFIRM (Cigna, Microsoft, North Korea supply-chain)
methodologyNotes.description: Cigna (20.3) confirmed at boundary position — 0.3 points above Critical — with claim-denial and algorithmic-review profile; AZ AG cartel suit is the active adjudication channel. Microsoft (65.3) confirmed with positive-watch active; HR Review Board operational but without verified contract-blocking output. North Korea forced-labor EU supply-chain documentation flagged as sector-wide monitoring baseline; no entity-specific Fortune 500 proposal generated this cycle — CSDDD audit obligation activation is the conversion trigger for any specific entity.

forwardTriggers.trigger: EU Returns Regulation enters force. Documented EU-facilitated return without individual assessment where a third-country national faces persecution constitutes a scored signal for the non-refoulement dimension. India at 15.6 is the primary watch entity; confirmed state-attributable enforced disappearances among deportees at scale would constitute a new harm category independently scorable beyond 15.6.

WARN Act last-working-day sign-or-forfeit window closes for approximately 30,000 affected workers. Adverse adjudication on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory in the Washington state class action or Missouri state investigation constitutes an immediate scoring trigger from 0.6 points above Critical.
Australia $1.43B PFAS Phase-2 MDL deadline. Adverse adjudication or settlement attributable to 3M directly crosses from 0.3 points above Critical into Critical.

Ebola Bundibugyo peak window through July. 598 cases / 115 deaths with Kampala urban escalation; 17-health-zone spread; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine. Documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response breaks the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold at the near-floor 2.3 composite.
Ebola Kampala urban cluster (nine cases) peak window through July. WHO-coordinated response active; documented state obstruction of WHO access is the crossing trigger from 0.3 points above Critical.

EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect. Fines up to 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover. No assessed AI lab has published compliance documentation — 53 days from this cycle. Anthropic IPO S-1 filing does not constitute compliance disclosure.

ICJ ruling in Sudan v. UAE (filed March 2025 alleging UAE complicity in genocide). Verified cessation of RSF arms and mercenary transfers is the primary reversal signal for the applied 18.4 Critical composite. Documented direct UAE-force participation in Sudan would move toward a floor assessment. Applied score reinforced by six independent documentation cycles as of June 10.

Judicial annulment or legislative repeal of the June 8 presumption-of-legality military-force law. Documented mass-casualty event under the signed law attributable to state forces constitutes a new harm category scorable beyond 18.4.

Appellate review of Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions. Reinstatement of the rescinded June 4 ICE post-release death-reporting requirement is the first upward-direction indicator. Continued non-reinstatement deepens the transparency-deficit record.

Verified Human Rights Review Board decision demonstrably blocking or exiting a harmful national-security contract — conversion trigger from positive-watch to scorable upgrade. Quiet reinstatement of terminated Unit 8200 access is a negative trigger.

Merits adjudication or settlement of any of the four converging sovereign enforcement actions — DOJ MA-billing criminal probe, AZ AG eight-insurer cartel suit, Lifepoint federal complaint, or DOJ Antitrust MultiPlan grand-jury subpoena. AZ AG adjudication would simultaneously reach seven other Fortune 500 health insurers.

DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth. Favorable ruling triggers Functional/Established crossing from 59.1; adverse ruling produces downward movement. IPO completion does not affect this trigger. Enacted Great American AI Act compliance documentation would be independently scorable.

Adjudicated claim-denial finding or mental-health parity ruling from 0.3 points above Critical. AZ AG eight-insurer cartel suit adjudication is the sector-wide channel reaching Cigna alongside seven other Fortune 500 health insurers.

Documented cessation of systematic civilian targeting of all four documented categories (schools, hospitals, residential infrastructure, religious sites) and verified accountability mechanism activation. Floor exits through demonstrated perpetration cessation, not through time.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: Does the Bago monastery airstrike — a fourth documented civilian target category for Myanmar — constitute sufficient evidence to activate an ICC referral pathway under the Rome Statute's protected-site provisions, and if so, which Security Council veto-holder configuration would determine whether a referral proceeds?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: Myanmar's 0.0 floor deepens with a fourth target category; the gap between evidentiary sufficiency for a floor and procedural access to accountability mechanisms is the live methodological question.
